audit_items.proof_trace: In Stop(), _timeout_manager.reset() (L115) is called before _maintain_thread_group->stop() (L119) and while _is_running is already false (L108). CallMethod() reads _is_running without holding _start_stop_lock, so an application thread calling CallMethod() after L115 will see _is_running==false, invoke cntl->Done() inline (L224), which calls DoneCallback, which unconditionally dereferences the now-null _timeout_manager at L414 (_timeout_manager->erase(...)) — a null shared_ptr dereference crash. The erase() method's id==0 guard is inside the method body and cannot protect against the null dereference of the pointer itself.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker controlling request timing to a server using sofa-pbrpc as an RPC client can send a request at the moment the client is shutting down, causing a concurrent CallMethod() invocation after _timeout_manager is nulled, triggering the null dereference and crashing the process.
